title: alchemilla alpestris var. cuspidens
intro: Alchemilla alpestris var. cuspidens, also known as alpine lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the alpine lady's mantle plant, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive problems, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. Alchemilla alpestris var. cuspidens can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
